name: Zamora and Sayago Region Loop
name_original: Zamora – vuelta por Bermillo de Sayago y Fresno de Sayago
description: Road cycling the Zamora – return via Bermillo de Sayago and Fresno de Sayago route covers 55.0 miles (88.5 km) with moderate difficulty.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Campos-Pan traverse the plains and river valleys of the Castilla y León region in Spain. The landscape features open agricultural fields, the Duero River, and several reservoirs, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. Routes often include gentle ascents and descents through rural areas, connecting historic towns and natural features. The region is characterized by its expansive, generally flat to rolling topography, suitable for sustained road cycling efforts. Best road cycling routes around Campos-Pan The most popular road cycling route is Zamora and Ricobayo Dam Loop, a 38.0 miles (61.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Zamora and Toro Loop, a moderate 62.1 miles (99.9 km) path. This route connects the historic towns of Zamora and Toro, often following the Duero River. Local road cyclists also love the Tierra de Campos Loop: Medina de Rioseco and Tordehumos, a 46.3 miles (74.5 km) trail leading through the agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Campos-Pan is defined by open plains, river valleys, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Campos-Pan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Campos-Pan region?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available in the Campos-Pan region, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Campos-Pan?
A: The road cycling routes around Campos-Pan primarily traverse the plains and river valleys of the Castilla y León region in Spain. You can expect open agricultural fields, views of the Duero River, and several reservoirs. The terrain generally features gentle ascents and descents through rural areas, suitable for sustained cycling efforts.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or families in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370633719/castillo-de-altafria-y-palacio-de-los-duques-de-alba-circular-desde-villanueva-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Altafria y Palacio de los Duques de Alba — circular desde Villanueva del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 26-mile (41.9 km) loop that offers a pleasant ride through the area.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of natural features and historic towns. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5508361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest&lt;/a&gt;. Routes often connect historic towns like Zamora and Toro, offering cultural sights along the way.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, many routes in Campos-Pan are designed as loops. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025489139/zamora-and-toro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora and Toro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Duero River, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370649524/tierra-de-campos-loop-medina-de-rioseco-and-tordehumos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Loop: Medina de Rioseco and Tordehumos&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through agricultural plains.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Campos-Pan?
A: While specific seasonal details for Castilla y León are not provided, regions with similar agricultural plains and river valleys often offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Campos-Pan?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 14 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from open plains to river valleys, and the scenic views of reservoirs and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, making it enjoyable for many.

Q: How long are the typical road cycling routes in this region?
A: Route lengths vary, but many popular routes are moderate in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369980791/zamora-and-ricobayo-dam-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora and Ricobayo Dam Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 38 miles (61.2 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1370239378/benavente-and-villafafila-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and Villafáfila Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 58 miles (93.8 km).

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes feature stunning views of lakes and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369993307/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-from-zamora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Zamora&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering extensive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve.

Q: What are the options for more advanced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: While many routes are moderate, some offer longer distances and more elevation gain for advanced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025417437/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-from-zamora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Zamora&lt;/a&gt; (a different version) is a challenging 62-mile (100.3 km) route with significant elevation changes, providing a good workout.

Q: Can I find routes that connect different towns or historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The routes in Campos-Pan often connect historic towns, allowing you to experience the region's cultural heritage while cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023159706/zamora-toro-duero-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora - Toro Duero River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, linking these two historic towns along the Duero River.
tips: One of the cultural centers of Zamora. The Zamora bullring was built by the city council in 1875 in a style reminiscent of the Neo-Mudéjar style typical of Spanish bullrings with a capacity for 10,000 seats. Celebration of an annual festival every June.

Although it was never a palatial castle but a fortress in which to protect itself and the city, it is the element that best defines the history of Zamora, receiving, in addition, the maximum protection within the Spanish Historical Heritage in 1931.
According to the chronicles, it was ordered to be built by Alfonso II, although studies carried out by historians show that it would actually be ordered to be built by Fernando I, so the building would date from the mid-11th century, although very few remains remain from this period.
The Poets Bridge is a modern bridge over the Duero River. The bridge gives you spectacular views of the city and the river landscape.

Small rural town of the municipality and Spanish town of the province of Zamora. There are some parking spaces near the Plaza Mayor in front of the town hall. Water fountain and beach bars to eat after a long day on the bike.

It spans the Duero River in Zamora (Castilla y León), connecting Avenida de Portugal (Pinilla neighborhood) with Calle de Salamanca in the city.
The project began in 1882, with construction starting in 1892, under the direction of engineer Prudencio Guadalajara, to alleviate the overloading of the old Stone Bridge.
Inaugurated in 1900, it represented a significant improvement in handling the growing traffic in the city.
It was designed in steel, using a metal lattice structure of the St. Andrew's Cross type, typical of the late 19th century.

faqs.question: Where does the racebike route Zamora – return via Bermillo de Sayago and Fresno de Sayago start?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts and ends in Zamora. While specific parking details are not provided, Zamora offers various parking options within the city, from where you can easily access the start of the tour.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain. Sections around Fresno de Sayago are described as 'llana y arcillosa' (flat and clayey), primarily agricultural. However, the wider Zamora province features diverse landscapes, so while some parts will be relatively flat, others may include gentle climbs. The route is generally suitable for racebikes.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of historical and natural sights. You'll pass by landmarks such as the Plaza de Toros de Zamora, the historic Castle of Zamora, and the impressive Puente de los Poetas. In Bermillo de Sayago, you can see the Plaza Mayor of Bermillo de Sayago, and the Puente de Hierro de Zamora is also along the way. Expect traditional rural landscapes and agricultural fields, especially around Fresno de Sayago.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' with an elevation gain of approximately 635 meters over 88.5 kilometers. While sections may be relatively flat, the overall distance and elevation make it more suitable for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners. Beginners might find it challenging.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and pleasant cycling conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter weather of winter. You'll also experience the rural landscapes at their most vibrant.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Ruta del Duero and Etapa 19: Zamora – Pereruela, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing through towns like Bermillo de Sayago and Fresno de Sayago, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and rest. These traditional villages offer a chance to experience local hospitality.

faqs.question: Is the Zamora – return via Bermillo de Sayago and Fresno de Sayago route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a racebike tour of this distance and nature is not typically recommended unless they are specifically trained for such an activity (e.g., running alongside or in a specialized trailer). Always ensure your dog's safety and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. You can enjoy the tour freely.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's distance and duration, it's advisable to pack essentials such as sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a charged phone. Depending on the season, consider layers for changing weather conditions. A GPS device or the komoot app on your phone is also recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this racebike tour is approximately 3 hours and 40 minutes (13,196 seconds) of active cycling time. This can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and the number of stops you make along the way.
